_doc: The Company's service registry — the single self-describing source of truth for what runs on this machine. The AI reads this to know the whole landscape; the `company` command (ops/company) operates it. Adding a service = adding an entry here. It's OK if some entries are imperfect (ports/units) — better than the scatter it replaces. systemd is the muscle underneath; this is the map + the console. NOTHING auto-starts at boot anymore (2026-06-06, Tim's call): every service is on-demand via `company up`. The `autostart` flag now ONLY means 'started by bare `company up` (no target)' — kept true for the surface (canvas+bridge) so `company up` brings the interactive Company up; everything else is explicit (`company up brain|voice|<svc>`). `vram_mb` is an approximate resident/reserved cost used by `company` to surface GPU budget against the 16 GB ceiling — estimates, not measured. `serve` is the script whose default model `company swap` rewrites.

combos._doc:
Named sets of services meant to run TOGETHER. `company up @<name>` starts the set (the resource manager still checks it all fits). Add a combo = add an entry here. Extensible — a combo can mix any services (models, voice, surface).
VARIANTS (the loadout-'class' mechanism, 2026-06-28): a combo can declare `extends: <base-combo>` to inherit the base's service list, then override just what differs — `swap: {old-svc: new-svc}`, `add: [svc]`, `remove: [svc]`. So a class is a base loadout (e.g. `interaction`) and its variants (e.g. `interaction-parakeet`) state only the difference. registry.combos() resolves this centrally (fail-loud on a missing base / a swap target not in the base / an unknown service / an extends-cycle), so both `company combos` and `up @<variant>` see the final concrete service list. Example: `interaction` (ear=stt-moonshine) and `interaction-parakeet` (extends interaction, swaps the ear to stt-parakeet-onnx) — same loadout, different ear, for trialling configurations.

small-pair.note: the 2B + 0.8B chat tiers co-resident (gpu_util 0.45 + 0.30 ≈ 12.3 GB — VERIFIED both served at once 2026-06-06, fits alongside voice).

wake.note: the after-restart wake ritual (Tim, 2026-06-11): the surface's API + the resident brain/embedder pair + the default ear/voice — everything the phone PWA needs. ~12GB co-resident, verified together 2026-06-11. NOTE: the canvas (vite) is unmanaged — start it separately (cd canvas/app && npm run dev); making it a managed unit is a named future row.

xsession.note: The cross-session CHANNEL fabric loadout (Tim gave the GPU 2026-06-14): pplx-embed-context-4b (~5.4G, 8-bit) for semantic session-find/transcript-search + Qwen3-TTS VoiceDesign (~4.4G, no clip — the voice for spoken cross-session conversation) + whisper.cpp STT (CPU, 0 VRAM, the ear) + rerank-jina (GPU, ~1.3G bf16 — the rerank stage for cross-session recall, moved to GPU 2026-06-28: warm ~0.05s vs ~16s CPU). ~11.1G GPU co-resident, fits the 16G card with headroom.

instrument.note: The INSTRUMENT surface loadout (Tim 2026-06-15: 'take the strongest, leverage everything'): the surface API (bridge :8770) + its embedder pplx-embed-context-v1-4b (~8.2G, 2560-dim, 32K ctx, context-aware/late-chunking — the strongest, replacing legacy BGE-M3). `company up @instrument` brings the surface up on pplx. Add voice (tts/stt) if the right-hand-man is co-resident. Leverage + tool-request spec: build-prep/embedder-pplx/LEVERAGE-AND-TOOL-REQUESTS.md.

xsession-brain.note: The cross-session fabric WITH a local brain (Tim 2026-06-15: 'the 4b co with lead … without the voice in order to fit the 4b'). xsession minus GPU voice (tts-qwen3tts) plus the local worker chat-4b — so the lead can run composable-mind / swarm turns on the local 4B WHILE the cross-session recall embedder stays resident. Footprint (2026-06-28): embed-pplx ~5.4G (8-bit) + chat-4b ~6.5G + rerank-jina ~1.3G (bf16 GPU) + stt-whisper (CPU ear) ≈ 13.2G / 16G, ~2.8G headroom. PRECONDITIONS persisted: chat-4b gpu_util 0.40 / max_model_len 16384 / max_num_seqs 4 (hybrid-Mamba: cache pool holds limited Mamba blocks). Add GPU voice only by dropping the 4b — they don't all fit at once.

interaction.note: INTERACTION loadout (class base, 2026-06-28): realtime conversation + Speech-To-Action with the local brain. Default ear = Moonshine (lean, CPU, 0 VRAM). embed-pplx+rerank for recall, chat-4b brain, tts-kokoro voice out. Swap the ear via the variants below.

interaction-parakeet.note: VARIANT of `interaction`: same loadout, Parakeet int8-ONNX ear instead of Moonshine (accurate + 25-language + hotword biasing). For trialling the two ears in the same configuration.

groups.core: The live interactive Company — the surface + its API (the always-on heart)
groups.models: On-demand models — started per task, VRAM-budgeted, usually dormant
groups.reach: Network + always-on system services (the body's connection to the world)
groups.jobs: Scheduled timers — derived-data builders (no port; ◐ active = timer armed, next fire on schedule)

pages.title: Page-face — serves a rendered HTML page bound to any address (separate origin, no-script CSP)
pages.note: On-demand (`company up pages`). DELIBERATELY a SEPARATE ORIGIN from the bridge control plane (:8770) so a served page can never same-origin-fetch /api; every page is served under a no-script CSP. A page is the visible face of ANY addressed thing (ui://, skill://, guide://, run://…), attached via inspect_address(op='attach_page'). Auth (who may reach :8774) rides the later Supabase move; separate-origin + no-script CSP hold regardless.

remote.title: Remote connector — public MCP front (Funnel→:8772; identity-gated: all tools for Tim, deny-all otherwise)

session-supervisor.title: Session supervisor — owns the supervised Claude Code fleet (Session Fabric F1)
session-supervisor.note: 127.0.0.1 ONLY (exposure law, audit B3); spawns claude subprocesses ON DEMAND (idle = owns nothing); the SINGLE writer of agent_sessions.* events; consumes agent_sessions/mail.jsonl intents. Cap COMPANY_FABRIC_CONCURRENCY (default 3) · per-turn watchdog COMPANY_FABRIC_TURN_TIMEOUT_S (default 900s) · permission COMPANY_FABRIC_PERMISSION (default plan). Operator face: `company session`. On-demand per the no-boot-autostart law.

chat-4b.title: Qwen3.5-4B-AWQ (vLLM) — a local WORKER (not the RHM brain; the RHM main is a cloud model)
chat-4b.note: config-driven via serve_model.sh (migrated from serve script 2026-06-06). Tune: `company config chat-4b <key> <value>`.

config._migration: flags[] RETIRED -> resolver generates from family qwen3.5 (--enable-prefix-caching --enable-auto-tool-choice --tool-call-parser qwen3_xml --reasoning-parser qwen3 --trust-remote-code). SHIPPING shape: dropped --chat-template nothink.jinja (uses the model's OWN stock thinking-capable template + per-request enable_thinking) + added --reasoning-parser qwen3 (the real reasoning fix). INTENDED CHANGE from the old AWQ flags (Tim: don't preserve nothink). gpu_util AUTO-ALLOCATED from _profile (no static literal): ~0.419 (5838 weights + 16384x31.7/1024 KV + 512 overhead)/16376 ceiling. OPEN: AWQ (cyankiwi) bundled-template thinking-capability UNVERIFIED (the spike verified the FP8/RedHatAI template) — verify enable_thinking works on this build before next launch.

chat-4b-fp8.title: Qwen3.5-4B-FP8 (vLLM) — the FP8 brain: thinking + tools, model's own template
chat-4b-fp8.note: config-driven via serve_model.sh. FP8 (RedHatAI) on Ada; --language-model-only (text RHM); model's OWN template (thinking-capable, per-request enable_thinking); --reasoning-parser qwen3 splits reasoning_content. The FP8 brain for the spike + C5. Tune: `company config chat-4b-fp8 <key> <value>`.

config._migration: flags[] RETIRED -> resolver generates from family qwen3.5 + capability_overrides.vision=lm-only. VERIFIED byte-for-byte == the prior flags[] (--enable-prefix-caching --enable-auto-tool-choice --tool-call-parser qwen3_xml --reasoning-parser qwen3 --language-model-only --trust-remote-code). vision=lm-only injects --language-model-only (text RHM skips the vision tower for VRAM) at order rank 40, between reasoning and trust_remote_code. SAFETY-CRITICAL: this is the LIVE brain on :8001 — a restart must be identical; the resolver reproduces it exactly. gpu_util 0.5 KEPT as the explicit static override (no _profile yet; interim co-resident value — measure a _profile to auto-allocate).

config._note: model-id NOT located on disk (kokoro runs but its weights id is unconfirmed) — left empty; flag for verification. Not cataloged (no verified id).

stt-whisper.title: Whisper.cpp STT (voice in — the boot-default ear)
stt-whisper.note: the live zero-install on-machine ear; serves the OpenAI-compat route /v1/audio/transcriptions (this build returns 404 for /inference). STT_DEFAULT=whispercpp. Registered in voice/stt.py STT_PROVIDERS.

stt-parakeet.title: NVIDIA Parakeet-TDT 0.6B v3 STT (GPU, NeMo)
stt-parakeet.note: managed as user-unit (flipped from manual 2026-06-06: cgroup teardown is orphan-safe; `company up/down` and voice/lifecycle agree). GPU ear — VERIFIED RUNNING 2026-06-05 (load→up ~30s, ~5.1 GB resident, transcribes; unload frees). Own NeMo venv (CUDA-13). Live multilingual workhorse → candidate STT_DEFAULT. See voice/ears/REQUIREMENTS.md.

stt-canary.title: NVIDIA Canary-Qwen 2.5B STT (GPU, NeMo)
stt-canary.note: managed as user-unit (flipped from manual 2026-06-06: cgroup teardown is orphan-safe; `company up/down` and voice/lifecycle agree). GPU ear — heaviest (~10 GB resident, near-OOM); on-demand only, never co-resident with a local brain. Own NeMo venv. English-max + understanding. See voice/ears/REQUIREMENTS.md.

embed-bge.title: BGE-M3 embeddings (vLLM)

config._migration: flags[] RETIRED -> resolver generates from family embed-pooling (--runner pooling --trust-remote-code). VERIFIED byte-for-byte == prior flags[]. gpu_util 0.3 KEPT as explicit static override (no _profile to auto-allocate from — measure one to dissolve).

embed-bge.note: config-driven via serve_model.sh (migrated from serve script 2026-06-06). Tune: `company config embed-bge <key> <value>`.

embed-jina-v4.title: jina-embeddings-v4 multimodal (vLLM)
embed-jina-v4.note: CUSTOM server — NOT config-driven. vLLM lacks jina-v4 support, so it runs serve_jina_v4.py (raw transformers) in the jina-v4-env venv via serve_jina_v4.sh. Leave script-based.

config._note: config.model = JOIN/identity metadata ONLY — CUSTOM transformers server (see `serve`/`note`), NOT config-driven (vLLM lacks jina-v4 support; serve_jina_v4.py raw transformers). CORRECTED from an earlier 'confirm vLLM --runner pooling serves it' note, which contradicted the working custom server.

embed-pplx.title: pplx-embed-context-v1-4b (custom transformers embedder)
embed-pplx.note: CUSTOM server — NOT config-driven (like embed-jina-v4). Arch PPLXQwen3Model / model_type bidirectional_pplx_qwen3 is remote-code (trust_remote_code, auto_map→modeling.PPLXQwen3ContextualModel); vLLM 0.21 has no such arch (verified: absent from 356 supported archs, `vllm serve` fails at load_model) → runs ops/serve_pplx_embed.py (raw transformers) in ~/vllm-env via serve_pplx_embed.sh. OpenAI-compat POST /v1/embeddings; dim=2560; output is INT8 + UNNORMALIZED — compare via COSINE (model card). Contextual late-chunking model: flat `input` strings are embedded as single-chunk docs; optional `documents` (list[list[str]]) gives native per-chunk contextual embeddings. dtype bfloat16 (~8GB) — the card stores fp32. Interim transcript-search embedder (throwaway); ~10GB load authorized by Tim. Leave script-based.

config._note: config.model = JOIN/identity metadata ONLY. This is a CUSTOM transformers server (see `serve`/`note`), deliberately NOT config-driven. The context-4b IS served — the live transcript-search embedder, wired + verified by the retrieval session 2026-06-12. Set here (catalog session) so capabilities_for() resolves served_by. CORRECTED from a wrong earlier value (pplx-embed-v1-0.6b + a 'launch UNVERIFIED' note) — it is the 4b and it serves.

litellm.title: LiteLLM proxy (OpenAI-compat gateway, :4100) — for the fabric's OWN cognition
litellm.note: The CURRENT clean proxy (litellm 1.83.7, .litellm-venv, fabric/litellm.config.yaml) — LOAD-BEARING for fabric cognition (fabric/config.py LITELLM_PROXY, OpenAI-format → ollama). Launched MANUALLY by fabric/serve_litellm.sh (no systemd unit). Corrected 2026-06-16: dropped the dangling 'litellm-proxy.service' manage ref (that was the STALE ~/claude-gateway proxy on :4000, now removed). ★ Claude Code does NOT use this — CC runs on company/ollama models via OLLAMA-NATIVE :11434 (ollama launch claude / the supervisor _provider_env), not this OpenAI proxy. board://item-4aa4f952.

ollama.title: Ollama runtime (GGUF big models)
ollama.note: system-unit, kept enabled at boot (Tim's call). 0 VRAM idle; loads a model on request.

tailscale.title: Tailscale (tailnet + HTTPS serve to phone)
tailscale.note: system-unit, kept enabled at boot — the phone access path.

pipeliner.title: GitHub Actions self-hosted runner (Concept-Vi/ollama-pipeliner)
pipeliner.note: CI runner. On-demand as of 2026-06-06 (was boot-autostart). `company up pipeliner` when a CI run is needed; `company down pipeliner` to free it.

agent-sessions-exporter.title: Claude session transcripts → ~/corpora/claude-sessions markdown (every 20 min)
agent-sessions-exporter.note: F1.4 transcript memory. Managed unit = the TIMER (`company up agent-sessions-exporter` arms it; the oneshot .service fires every 20 min). READ-ONLY on ~/.claude; filter law + redaction in ops/agent_sessions_exporter.py. Timer is WantedBy=company.target so it rises/falls with the Company (no orphan). The SECOND stage of the transcript-search circuit (export → reindex) is `claude-sessions-reindex`.

claude-sessions-reindex.title: Reindex beat — substrate DELTA reindex of claude-sessions after each export (~5 min offset)
claude-sessions-reindex.note: INTERIM transcript-search (throwaway; Tim is building the real memory system elsewhere). The SECOND beat of the circuit: Claude jsonl → [agent-sessions-exporter] → corpus markdown → [this] → searchable index. Managed unit = the TIMER (WantedBy=company.target; fires *:05/20, ~5 min after the exporter's *:00/20). The oneshot .service runs ops/claude_sessions_reindex.py in the substrate venv (obsidian-overlord/.venv). CHEAP-BY-DESIGN: compares the corpus' newest .md mtime + file count against ~/corpora/claude-sessions/.reindex_marker.json and EXITS doing nothing (no model load, no GPU) when nothing changed — the common case. When transcripts changed it runs a substrate DELTA scan (sha256 skips unchanged files) of the `claude-sessions` vault embedded by the `embed-pplx` service into an ISOLATED state dir (~/.cache/company/substrate-claude-sessions, separate from the bge-m3 substrate). FAIL LOUD: if files changed but embed-pplx is DOWN it exits non-zero and leaves the marker stale so the next fire retries (`company up embed-pplx` first, or it can self-ensure with --ensure-embedder). Search face: ops/wire_substrate_claude_sessions.py search / ops/transcript_search.py (numpy fallback).

rerank-jina.title: jina-reranker-v3 listwise reranker (GPU endpoint)
rerank-jina.note: GPU endpoint (COMPANY_RERANK_DEVICE=cuda, 2026-06-28 Tim — listwise rerank is attention-bound, CPU was ~16s on realistic chunks; GPU warm ~0.05s). Override COMPANY_RERANK_DEVICE=cpu to pin to CPU (0 VRAM). Surfaces ops/rerank.py:Reranker(jina-v3) as POST /rerank {query, candidates|texts:[str|dict], top_n?} -> {ranking:[{item,text,rerank_score,orig_rank,rank},...]} sorted desc; GET /health. Runs from ~/vllm-env via serve_rerank.sh. ~1.3GB (bf16 on GPU; fp32 was ~2.8GB); weights jinaai/jina-reranker-v3 in HF cache. The rerank stage for cross-session semantic recall (runtime/session_recall.py) + the determine pipeline (runtime/recall_determine.py).

remote-gateway.title: Connector gateway — stable cloud OAuth door (Supabase edge fn) forwarding ALL apps → Funnel → :8772 remote connector
remote-gateway.note: thin OAuth forwarder — no tool logic, no access decision; the public door for ALL apps (name claude-design is a misnomer)

stt-moonshine.title: Moonshine STT (ONNX, compact realtime ear)
stt-moonshine.note: The LEANEST ear: ONNX-runtime (no torch/NeMo), <1GB, built for low-latency LIVE conversation + Speech-To-Action. Runs CPU by default (~9x realtime, ~0.3-0.5s/short-turn on this box) so it co-resides with the brain+embedder with zero VRAM cost. Doesn't pad short clips (fast on commands). English-focused. Model COMPANY_MOONSHINE_MODEL (default moonshine/base; v2-medium ~Whisper-large accuracy is a drop-in env swap). Tuning: built-in IntentRecognizer (semantic command->action), vad_threshold, max_tokens_per_second. The default interaction-loadout ear.

stt-parakeet-onnx.title: Parakeet-TDT 0.6B v3 int8 ONNX (sherpa-onnx, lean)
stt-parakeet-onnx.note: The QUANTIZED lean Parakeet: int8 ONNX on sherpa-onnx (no NeMo). ~1-1.5GB RAM, CPU by default (int8 sweet spot, 0 VRAM) so it co-resides like Moonshine. Accurate (~6.3% WER) + 25 EUROPEAN languages w/ auto-detect. Supports HOTWORD/CONTEXT BIASING (boost UI terms/command words — the STA payoff, up to thousands of phrases). Set COMPANY_PARAKEET_ONNX_DEVICE=cuda for the GPU provider. The parakeet variant of the interaction loadout class. (Distinct from stt-parakeet, the heavy NeMo/GPU build.)
